summary: Comprehensive frontend testing of 999 Taxi landing (Uzbek rebuild). All sections render with correct data-testids, all nav/hero/service/final/footer CTAs have correct hrefs (tel:+998781139991 and https://t.me/t_boxodir). 3D hero canvas renders. FAQ accordion opens/closes correctly. LeadForm submission triggers window.open to https://t.me/t_boxodir?text=... with pre-filled Uzbek message and shows success state with 'Rahmat!'; reset returns to form. Mobile viewport (390x844): sticky bottom bar visible with all 3 CTAs, hamburger toggle opens a full-screen menu with all Uzbek links (visually verified via screenshot), no horizontal overflow. Desktop scrolls vertically, no horizontal overflow. No console errors. No leftover English strings ('Order now','Download app','How it works').

issues: Full-screen mobile menu container lacks data-testid='mobile-menu' attribute (menu itself works and displays all Uzbek links + CTAs). Consider adding for cleaner test hooks.

action_items: Optional: add data-testid='mobile-menu' to the mobile menu container in Navigation.jsx for test consistency (functionality unaffected).
critical_code_review_comments: Landing.jsx is thin and composition-only — clean.
All CTAs verified to use consistent tel:/t.me links from brand constants.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend-only project (no /api backend). Preview URL from frontend/.env. LeadForm submission uses window.open to Telegram — intercept via page.on('popup'). All sections/testids verified per review_request spec.
